question: how to mgen the laptop
answer:
According to the context, to MGEN the laptop:

1. Run workflow: "fusion rack configuration" tab then the 4th workflow.
2. PPA machine type, model and serial number (MMMM-TTT SSSSSSS) --> 9855-E03 PPRACK4
3. PPA Component ID -> 5900BIR00
4. Then run node discovery workflow to populate the ‘manufacturer’ field
5. Then run the kickstart workflow
6. Then move to stage 2 tunnel
context:
(Example using isf-2.10.0 firmware stack)

chmod 755 /data/maintenance/firmware/isf-2.10.0

chmod 755 /data/maintenance/firmware/isf-2.10.0/rhelchmod 755 /data/maintenance/firmware/isf-2.10.0/prov-baseos

[Dhanashree]:

For PPA (Passport Advantage) configuration: 

Run workflow:   

- IMP:  This workflow should be run at between step 5(run IMM)  and 6 (Kickstart) 

- Workflow to run:  "fusion rack configuration" tab then the 4th workflow



PPA machine type, model and serial number (MMMM-TTT SSSSSSS) --> 9855-E03 PPRACK4     (note PPRACK4 is 7 digit)

PPA Component ID -> 5900BIR00





then run node discovery workflow to populate the ‘manufacturer’ field

then run the kickstart workflow 





then move to stage 2 tunnel

root@fd8c:215d:178e:c0de:bace:f6ff:fe01:c07e: Permission denied (publickey,gssapi-keyex,gssapi-with-mic,password).

RCA: run below command 

 ssh-keygen -R fd8c:215d:178e:c0de:bace:f6ff:fe01:c07e 

Issue 1: Not able to open vnc 



try opening on port 1-4 ports if still doesnt work ,

then kill all the vnc process using command 

ps -aef | grep vnc   (from here u will get process id)

kill -9 <process-id>     ( it will kill all the sessions of the vnc)

vncserver             ( it will start the vnc server and show the port also)

from below picture you can check vncserver is open on port 3 



Issue2: when not able to open firebox in vnc to access node console

run command in mgen laptop cli : killall firefox

Issue3: artifactory token expired 



RCA - login to mgen laptop ,

go to /data/purescale/gsa/mgen/laptops/roadien03/builds/ and run below commands

scp -r root@9.42.47.250:/home/manish/isf.mgen-x86_linux-2.10.0-20250312-0446-151.zip

to copy it from other racks 

go to the directory where build is present

scp -r  root@9.42.142.64:/data/purescale/gsa/mgen/laptops/roadien03/builds/isf.mgen-x86_linux-2.9.0-20241010-1402-954.zip

cd /data/purescale/gsa/mgen/laptops/roadien03/builds/

  Note:- roadien03 is rack name and it will be different for different rack so select accordingly .do ls to check if your required build is there or not

2. unzip -d /tmp isf.mgen-x86_linux-2.10.0-20250312-0446-151.zip

  Note:- isf.mgen-x86_linux-2.10.0-20250312-0446-151.zip is the build name you want to use.

3. chmod +x /tmp/ipas.mgen/bin/install.sh

4. /tmp/ipas.mgen/bin/install.sh

5. ps -aef | grep install.sh 

Note:- wait till 'ps' command stop showing install.sh. This may take a minute or 2.



6. cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen

./zero stop; ./zero start

If we get error of Launching of the ZSO failed with return code -1

run ps -aef | grep java , then kill java process using

kill -9 <pid> , pid= process id 

retry command ./zero stop; ./zero start

Launch/refresh MGEN UI from browser



Step 3: DOWNLOAD THE ISO 

Login to Mgen Laptop

# ssh root@<MGEN_IP>

Go to the ISF directory to download the provisioner and min ISO



go to the firmware where u want to download iso



cd /data/maintenance/firmware/isf-2.9.0/

To Download Provisioner ISO

, when we trigger cleanup imm_reconfig

 workflows → kickstart and rackinfo json → stage1 tunnel → restore tor configurations and reload ->restore merion configuration

Make sure if you are doing mgen of switches run Tor-backup configurations workflow instead of tor restore and reload workflow after mgen of switches. 



9. MAC address update in DHCP server:

To verify Mac Address of nodes with dhcp/dns server,

*****whenever we change the storage type and mgen the nodes/tor we need to update the new mac address in dhcpd.conf file 

follow below steps:

match the mac address of RackC_adapterCardMacs.txt with dhcp/dns files , and update dhcp/dns server if required 



In your dhcp server update the mac address as in this document link

https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/storage-fusion/2.6?topic=appliance-setting-up-dhcp



10.Imp Points related to Stage1 

if stage1 fails at 0% after trying at least 2 times( sometimes on retry it passed)

check if provisioner is able to ping dhcp server vi /etc/resolv.conf , from there you will get the gateway of dhcp server try pinging that ip from provisioner(rhel_ru23/rhel_ru7) , if not pinging 

then check if dhcp and dns service is running or not by using command(this command should be run in mgen laptop not in provisioner(rhel_ruX))systemctl status dhcpd.service    ( for dhcp)

systemctl status named.service       ( for dns)

if not running , execute above command with restart inplace of status and check again it starts or not

Issue3: artifactory token expired 



RCA - login to mgen laptop ,

 cd /data/maintanence/firmware/isf-<firm-id>/prov-baseos/

here isf-<firm-id> is the firmware stack u r using in the rack

here update metadata.json file "artifactoryCred" parameter with new artifactory token , you can generate a new token and use it here. then retry kickstart workflow again

Issue 4: restore merion workflow failed



when we perform cleanup of merion switch and we need to run below workflow to get the backup of its configuration . this backup file we store in the mgen laptop itself . this configuration json file we use during running restore merion workflow . so , when we doesn’t take backup /or run below workflow after merion mgen . restore merion workflow will failin that case either run below workflow than try restore merion workflow again or just ignore this failed workflow

(ARC 2) Switches: Backup Configuration

Issue 5: 



RCA : retry workflow again

Issue 6:



RCA: Run Ist workflow "MGEN is using interface eno2" from Mgen ui to assign ipv4 to interface and retry workflow again

Issue7: sometimes bmc stuck, so reboot the bmc with command spreset



RCA : login to mgen laptop

     cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen. 

     . <(./zero bashsetup)            ( to create alias will take a bit to execute)

      imm_ruX     ( login to the node where raid failed , X is node no.)

     spreset         (to reboot the node,will take some time to reboot)

      exit

e.g you can run gsa sync(if fw/build is not there which u required then copy ) in rackL else directly  copy it from rackL to your rack using scp command(use reverse scp here since this rack has public network)

if scp doesnt work from one rack to another , copy it in your local and upload it into your respective rack where you need it.



to copy firmware from rackL

reverse scp (run command from your rack where u want to copy)

scp -r root@9.42.142.93:/data/maintenance/firmware/isf-2.9.0/  .

for normal scp ( run in the rack from where u want to copy from)scp -r isf-2.9.0 root@9.42.45.5:/data/maintenance/firmware/



firmware stack should be copied in 

       cd /data/maintenance/firmware/ 

     after copying you can select the particular firmware from the            Mgen UI as shown in the above images.

firmware build should be copied in the mgen path

     cd /data/purescale/gsa/mgen/laptops/roadien03/builds/     note- roadien03 is the rack name and it will be different for  different racks and u will get its name from terminal only

     e.g here rack name is isfmgen9

Step 2 : Update/Upgrade mgen build: 

mgen build will look like “isf.mgen-x86_linux-2.9.0-20241128-1031-218.zip”

if build is in rack 9.42.142.93 and path /home/manish

run below command in the rack where u want to copy

to copy build from rackL

go to /data/purescale/gsa/mgen/laptops/roadien03/builds/ and run below commands

question: digest filw download failed
answer:
According to the provided context, the solution for the issue "artifactory token expired/ copying json file to provisional node / digest file download failed" is:

step 1: rca - login to mgen laptop
step 2: cd /data/maintanence/firmware/isf-<firm-id>/prov-baseos/
step 3: update metadata.json file "artifactorycred" parameter with new artifactory token, you can generate a new token and use it here.
then retry kickstart workflow again

So, the answer for your question is:

Update metadata.json file "artifactorycred" parameter with new artifactory token.
context:
step 3 : here instead of rhel-8.9-x86_64-provisioner.iso , you hv to pass your iso name , which u get by running ls command there  step 4 :  then retry workflow again.  issue : workflow failed at connection or command in incorrect disk solution : step 1 : rca: mtm of this node can be wrong in node_discovery.json file run below command to find correct mtm of the node e.g node2, then update correct mtm in node_discovery.json  step 2 : filedelete .md5 file and regenerate it using below command step3 : : cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen step 4 :  . <(./zero bashsetup) ( to create alias will take a bit to execute) step3 : : lla_imm_ru2  issue : workflow failed at raid configuration on imm or timeout solution : step 1 : rca: error message shows timeout , so retry workflow only  issue : most common error which occur when we add additional node / workflow failed at kickstart and rackinfo json solution : step 1 : rca: cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen step 2 : . <(./zero bashsetup) ( to create alias will take a bit to execute) step3 : : rackjson step 4 :  cat kickstart.json | grep null ( it will shows all the null values parameter in kickstart) step 5  : mostly it will be missing ibm serial no. for that run below workflow from mgen ui ( ibm serial number of rack components) .on running, it will ask for some

value to enter which u will get from kickstart.json file , that will be in current directory only, after completion of this workflow ,retry kickstart again.  issue : cluster not accessible ,check tunnel commands to check tunnel solution : step 1 : this means that tunnel is not enable to enable tunnel use below steps step 2 : ps -aef | grep ssh step 3 :  grep -i vip ~/ipi-installer/inventory/isf_hosts step 4 : ssh -n -f -l :6443:<apivip>:6443 root@localhost step 5 : ssh -n -f -l :443:<ingressvip>:443 root@localhost step 6 : ps -aef | grep ssh  issue : if workflow stuck at some step for vinery long time and not progressing (let it stuck at step2 ) solution : step 1 : rca: follow below steps, there you will find workflow name file , just update that file workflow_status parameter “failed” . then restart the ui using command step 2 : ./zero stop; ./zero start ( from path /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen) step 3 : refresh mgen ui from browser, it will make that workflow failed then u can trigger that workflow again step 4 : cd/data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen step 5 : . <(./zero bashsetup) step 4 : rackjson step 5 :  ls step 6 : cd dashboard/ step 7 : ls

issue : restore merion workflow failed solution : step 1 : when we perform cleanup of merion switch and we need to run below workflow to get the backup of its configuration . this backup file we store in the mgen laptop itself  step 2 : this configuration json file we use during running restore merion workflow .  so , when we doesn’t take backup /or run below workflow after merion mgen . restore merion workflow will fail  step3 in that case either run below workflow than try restore merion workflow again or just ignore this failed workflow step 4 : (arc 2) switches: backup configuration  issue : kickstart and rackinfo json file workflow failed solution : step  : retry workflow again  issue : install rhel on nodes workflow is failed solution : step  : rca: run ist workflow "mgen is using interface eno2" from mgen ui to assign ipv4 to interface and retry workflow again  issue :  bmc stuck, so reboot the bmc with command spreset / workflow failed at node ru – raid configuration solution : step 1 : rca : login to mgen laptop step 2 : cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen (go to this directory) step 3 : . <(./zero bashsetup) ( to create alias will take a bit to execute) step 4 : imm_rux ( login to the node where raid failed , x is node no.) step 5 : spreset (to reboot the node,will take some time to reboot)

step 6 : exit , then retry workflow again (try after sometime when u are able to login imm_rux again after reboot)  issue : error: marvell raid configuration can't be performed in current power state, please wait until uefi post end solution : step 1 : is because the node is booting or trying to booting and in this moment the node does not apply any change, and the easier way is booting to uefi to be available to work with it step 2 : power off  / power on / power uefi  step 3 : run commands in the imm , and wait to complete the boot until it show setup step 4 : system> power state              power on             state:system running in setup step 5: re-run the workflow, if still fails again then uefi might stuck, then need to reboot the node from remote console.  issue : password of ceuser and isfuser are lost /workflow failed at imm user configuration  solution : step 1 : update the node json file with correct password that u get from secrets that are saved while creating cluster or remove entry of ceuser and isfuser from its json file.( follow below commands) step 2 : cd/data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen step 3 : . <(./zero bashsetup) step 4 : rackjson step 5 :  ls step 6 : cd sr645 (folder name sr645 will change depands on node type it can be sr645/sr630/sr665) step 7 : ls

issue : host key verification failed solution : step 1 : run command (ssh-keygen -r <ip>) step 2 : [root@isfmgen1 prov-baseos]# rhel_ru2 : password is passw0rd step 3 : rca: run below command : ssh-keygen -r fd8c:215d:178e:c0de:bace:f6ff:fe01:c07e    issue : not able to open vnc / connection was refused by the computer solution : step 1 : try opening on port 1-4 ports if still doesnt work ,then kill all the vnc process using command step 2 :ps -aef | grep vnc (from here u will get process id) step3 : kill -9 <process-id> ( it will kill all the sessions of the vnc) step 4 : vncserver ( it will start the vnc server and show the port also)  issue : when not able to open firebox in vnc to access node console solution : step  : run command in mgen laptop cli : killall firefox  issue : artifactory token expired/ copying json file to provisional node /digest file download failed solution : step 1 : rca - login to mgen laptop , step 2 : cd /data/maintanence/firmware/isf-<firm-id>/prov-baseos/ step3 : here isf-<firm-id> is the firmware stack u r using in the rack step 4 : update metadata.json file "artifactorycred" parameter with new artifactory token , you can generate a new token and use it here. then retry kickstart workflow again

issue : raid step of imm_config/imm_reconfig fails with below error solution : step 1 : rca: cd /data/purescale/smash/ipas.mgen step 2 : . <(./zero bashsetup) ( to create alias will take a bit to execute) step 3 : imm_rux ( login to the node where raid failed , x is node no.) step 4 : power off / power on and power uefi (wait to complete the boot, will take some time and check power state , if it shows “setup” step 5 :  then retry workflow again.  issue :  workflow failed at json file copy to provisional node / filenotfoundexception /tmp/rackae3_order.json solution : step 1 : rca: in mgen ui , guadalajara_shadowisf is selected site, where rack_order.json file is not there.so, workaround is just create same file in mgen laptop  from root /tmp/rackae3_order.json using touch command step 2 : cd /tmp /tr step 3 : touch rackae3_order.json  issue : workflow failed at imm user solution : step 1 : retry workflow again  issue : md5 got corrupt and it shows gibrish(rarely occur) solution : step 1 : then go that path which is mentioned in the error message  delete .md5 file and regenerate it using below command step 2 : md5sum rhel-8.9-x86_64-provisioner.iso > rhel-8.9-x86_64-provisioner.md5

question: how to update firmware stack
answer:
To update the firmware stack:

1. Go to MGEN laptop and navigate to `/data/maintanence/firmware/isf-<firm-id>/prov-baseos/` directory.
2. Update the `metadata.json` file with the new Artifactory token, which can be generated and used here.
3. Then, retry the Kickstart workflow again.

To update the firmware from UI itself:

1. Run the MGEN workflow.
2. It will open a new tab.
3. Select the FW level from dropdown options and click OK.
4. The firmware will be updated.

Note: Make sure the firmware stack and build are in the same level before updating the firmware.

question: how to update serialconnection for mgen of tor switch
answer:
Based on the provided context, I found the following information that might be helpful:

To update the serial connection for MGEN of Tor switch, you can follow these steps:

1. Check if the firmware stack and build are in the same level (e.g., if fw required is 2.8.0, 2.8.1 or 2.8.2, make sure the build is also in 2.8.0). You can check the firmware stack currently running from UI.

2. If the firmware and build are not in the same level, you need to update the firmware stack to match the build. Make sure both are in the same level before proceeding.

3. To update the firmware, follow these steps:
   - Step 1: Update the firmware stack.
   - Check if the firmware required is 2.8.0, 2.8.1 or 2.8.2 and make sure the build is also in 2.8.0.

Please note that this information is based solely on the provided context, and I do not have any additional knowledge to provide.

question: mgen TOR switches : ( one time needed or whenever  there is storageType change from ece to fdf or vice-versa)
answer:
Here's the answer:

• Run mgen TOR switches: (one time needed or whenever there is storageType change from ece to fdf or vice-versa)

This instruction is explicitly stated in the context as follows:

"3. mgen tor switches : ( one time needed or whenever
there is storagetype change from ece to fdf or vice-versa)"
context:
remove existing iso using
rm -f rhel-9.2g1-x86_64-provisioner.iso rhel-9.2g1-x86_64-provisioner.md5
then download prov iso using command

wget
https://na.artifactory.swq-devops.com/artifactory/hyc-abell-devops-team-dev-generic-loca

visf-provisioner/9223016-2.7.1/rhel-8.6-x86_64-provisioner.iso --user=<w3id>@ibm.com
--ask-password

you will get download iso link from git ticket.

2. select storagetype from fusion rack configuration
section of mgen ui:

mgen is using storage type ece for configuring the rack network. if this is incorrect, click the button below to re-run the workflow and selec

run storagetype |

3. mgen tor switches : ( one time needed or whenever
there is storagetype change from ece to fdf or vice-versa)

note: -make sure if you are doing mgen of switches run tor-backup configurations
workflow instead of tor restore and reload workflow after mgen of switches.
